157.reason: // The program only records the error line but does not stop or skip further processing; moreover, it fails to handle potential index out-of-bounds when pos+1 exceeds sourceCode length, and it doesn't consume the rest of the token properly. Most critically, after detecting a single '&' or '|', it should report error 'a' immediately and terminate lexical analysis per problem statement (each erroneous example has exactly one lexical error), but instead continues parsing, which may lead to undefined behavior or missed error reporting.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  // The program only records the error line but does not stop or skip further processing; moreover, it fails to handle potential index out-of-bounds when pos+1 exceeds sourceCode length, and it doesn't consume the rest of the token properly. Most critically, after detecting a single '&' or '|', it should report error 'a' immediately and terminate lexical analysis per problem statement (each erroneous example has exactly one lexical error), but instead continues parsing, which may lead to undefined behavior or missed error reporting.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The program only records the error line but does not stop or skip further processing; moreover, it fails to handle potential index out-of-bounds when pos+1 exceeds sourceCode length, and it doesn't consume the erroneous character properly in all cases. Most critically, after detecting a single '&' or '|', it increments pos by 1 but continues parsing, which may lead to incorrect tokenization or missing the fact that this is a lexical error that should terminate normal token output. Additionally, the specification requires outputting error code 'a' for such cases, but the logic here sets ErrorLine yet still allows TokenList to possibly contain invalid tokens or continue scanning, and more importantly, it does not break out of the loop or prevent further token generation, violating the requirement that erroneous inputs produce only an error file.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The program only records the error line but does not stop processing or skip the invalid single '&' or '|' character properly; moreover, it fails to handle potential index-out-of-bounds when pos+1 exceeds sourceCode length. Additionally, after detecting the error, it continues parsing instead of breaking or ensuring no further tokenization occurs, which may lead to incorrect behavior or missed errors.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  // The program only records the error line but does not stop or skip the invalid single '&' or '|' properly; it continues processing, which may cause further issues. More critically, it fails to ensure that pos+1 is within bounds before accessing sourceCode.charAt(pos + 1), leading to possible StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the lexer should report an error and stop further tokenization, but it only records the error line and continues processing, which may lead to incorrect behavior or missing the error output if later tokens overwrite ErrorLine or if multiple errors occur. However, per problem statement, there's exactly one lexical error, so the main issue is that after detecting the error, the loop continues instead of breaking or skipping the rest. More critically, the code does not ensure that pos+1 is within bounds before accessing sourceCode.charAt(pos + 1), which can cause an IndexOutOfBoundsException when '&' or '|' appears at the end of file.]
